message:
perf(vdbe): skip high-page page-one probe

bd-db300.5.2

@@ -1626,7 +1626,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","title":"E1.1: map pipeline stages ownership boundaries and shared-versus-local state","description":"## Goal
Map the many-core transaction pipeline into stages with explicit ownership boundaries and shared-versus-local state placement.

## Checklist
1. Map the end-to-end transaction pipeline into stages with explicit ownership transitions and shared-state touch points.
2. Classify which state should stay lane-local, NUMA-local, socket-local, or globally shared on 32-plus-core hardware.
3. Make correctness invariants and reclamation boundaries explicit enough for follow-on implementation work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The pipeline is broken into stages with explicit ownership transitions and shared-state touch points.
- State placement is classified lane-local, NUMA-local, socket-local, or global.
- Correctness and reclamation boundaries are explicit enough for follow-on implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The pipeline is broken into stages with explicit ownership transitions and shared-state touch points. 2. State placement is classified lane-local, NUMA-local, socket-local, or global. 3. Correctness and reclamation boundaries are explicit enough for follow-on implementation.","notes":"This bead is the mechanical-sympathy map for the future architecture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:03:23.865266162Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:40:10.649210122Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:40:10.648625717Z","close_reason":"Documented many-core transaction pipeline stages, state placement, and invariants in docs/design/many-core-transaction-pipeline-state-placement.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","alien-graveyard","architecture","db300-program","db300-track-e","mechanical-sympathy","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.1.2","title":"E1.2: compare share-nothing lanes tiny-publish shared state and hierarchical hybrid designs","description":"## Goal
Compare share-nothing lanes, tiny-publish shared state, and hierarchical hybrid designs using real many-core tradeoffs.

## Checklist
1. Compare a pinned share-nothing lane design, a tiny-publish shared-state design, and a hierarchical hybrid shaped around sockets or NUMA domains.
2. Evaluate queueing, backpressure, memory traffic, failure modes, and operator ergonomics on Threadripper or Xeon class machines.
3. Keep rejected options visible so future work understands why they lost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bead compares the main candidate architectures explicitly.
- Queueing, backpressure, memory traffic, failure modes, and ergonomics are compared directly.
- Rejected options remain visible with reasons.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead compares share-nothing lanes, tiny-publish shared state, and a hierarchical hybrid design. 2. Queueing, backpressure, memory traffic, failure modes, and operator ergonomics are compared explicitly. 3. Rejected options remain visible with reasons.","notes":"This is where the Threadripper/Xeon-specific topology reasoning becomes concrete rather than aspirational.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:03:39.090911526Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:44:15.985798664Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:44:15.985115405Z","close_reason":"Compared share-nothing, tiny-publish, and hierarchical hybrid architectures in docs/design/many-core-architecture-comparison.md with queueing, backpressure, memory traffic, failure modes, and ergonomics made explicit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","alien-graveyard","architecture","db300-program","db300-track-e","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.1.3","title":"E1.3: choose the recommended many-core architecture and write the verification-ready decision record","description":"## Goal
Choose the recommended many-core architecture and record the resulting verification-ready decision.

## Checklist
1. Select the architecture that best balances scale, latency protection, durability semantics, and implementation tractability.
2. Record the expected many-core win mechanism, the residual serialized region, and the follow-on verification obligations.
3. Keep the output directly usable by the implementation beads without reopening architecture ambiguity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The chosen architecture names the expected scale mechanism and residual serialized region.
- Durability, latency protection, and tractability tradeoffs are explicit.
- Follow-on verification obligations are attached to the decision record.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The chosen architecture names the expected scale mechanism and residual serialized region. 2. Durability, latency protection, and tractability tradeoffs are explicit. 3. Follow-on verification obligations are attached to the decision record.","notes":"The output should remove architecture ambiguity for the implementation tracks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:03:54.255751242Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:47:07.293154268Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:47:07.292568491Z","close_reason":"Recorded the many-core architecture decision in docs/adr/0002-many-core-architecture-selection.md and docs/decision_register.jsonl with explicit scale mechanism, residual serialized region, and verification obligations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","architecture","db300-program","db300-track-e","evidence","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.5.2","title":"E2: prototype split-phase transactions with tiny publish","description":"## Goal
Prototype split-phase transactions that perform as much prepare, decode, and serialize work as possible before the final publish step, explicitly shrinking the residual serialized region to something tiny and measurable. Where that residual region is truly tiny and evidence supports it, this bead may evaluate optional HTM/lock-elision or queue/cohort-style fast paths with mandatory fallback, never as a correctness dependency.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A prototype path exists that separates parallelizable preparation from final publication and measures the serialized portion in instructions, cycles, and wall time.
2. Any residual serialized region has an explicit primitive and rationale (`plain lock`, `MCS/cohort-style handoff`, `HTM fast path with hard fallback`, or equivalent) instead of being left implicit.
3. The prototype is benchmarked on the canonical matrix and at least one adversarial hot cell, with throughput, `p50/p95/p99` latency, and fallback-or-abort-rate evidence where applicable.
4. Correctness and visibility rules remain explicit and tested.
5. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
6. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A prototype path exists that separates parallelizable preparation from final publication and measures the serialized portion in instructions, cycles, and wall time.
2. Any residual serialized region has an explicit primitive and rationale (`plain lock`, `MCS/cohort-style handoff`, `HTM fast path with hard fallback`, or equivalent) instead of being left implicit.
3. The prototype is benchmarked on the canonical matrix and at least one adversarial hot cell, with throughput, `p50/p95/p99` latency, and fallback-or-abort-rate evidence where applicable.
4. Correctness and visibility rules remain explicit and tested.
5. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
6. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead should aim for the smallest publish step that is still obviously correct. Optional HTM or lock-elision ideas are only acceptable as best-effort accelerators with an always-correct fallback, never as a new correctness assumption.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:02:06.362001487Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:43:44.736546126Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","concurrency","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-e","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2452,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2","author":"TealStream","text":"TealStream perf investigation 2026-03-18: current evidence still makes tiny-publish the highest-EV next step. On `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys / c8`, the deep profile shows ~95% of samples in `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page`, ~5.7 CPUs utilized, ~47.1M instructions/op, and IPC ~0.05, which is wasted parallelism rather than lack of cores. The current first-touch write loop in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` (`write_page_tier1_first_touch` / `write_page_tier2_commit_surface_rare`) repeatedly does handle locking, `CommitIndex::latest`, page-lock arbitration, wait/restore, then page-byte clone+stage before pager write. In parallel, `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/write_coordinator.rs` still centralizes FCW validation/update under a write lock on `commit_page_index`, and `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs` still has a serialized-writer drain path that polls `total_lock_count()` with 1 ms sleeps.

Recommendation: land E2.1/E2.2 as a real split-phase prepare/publish prototype and instrument the residual serialized region directly. Reuse the repo's existing building blocks rather than inventing new ones: the fast-array `CommitIndex`, `SeqLock`, `RcuCell`, and LeftRight primitives are already present. Fallback trigger: if direct Stage 4/5 timing shows publish/visibility work is <~5-10% of wall time and retry causes are mostly structural/page-1 rather than publication, re-rank effort toward E5/I4 instead of deepening the metadata-publication work.","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:43:44Z"}]}

instrument residual serialized publish region

## Checklist
1. Measure the remaining serialized publish region in cycles, instructions, and wall time.
2. Identify what work still sits inside the region.
3. Keep the instrumentation ready for prototype comparisons.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Measure the remaining serialized publish region in cycles, instructions, and wall time.
2. Identify what work still sits inside the region.
3. Keep the instrumentation ready for prototype comparisons.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Measure the remaining serialized publish region in cycles, instructions, and wall time.

3. Keep the instrumentation ready for prototype comparisons.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:18:41.454760999Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T16:21:21.566405323Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T16:21:21.565927839Z","close_reason":"Residual publish-region and mvcc_commit_surface instrumentation/reporting are already landed in engine.rs/perf_runner.rs/realdb_e2e; verified on current HEAD with rch cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --lib hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts -- --nocapture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-e","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2457,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18):
- Instrumentation/reporting slice from the prior pass is landed in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`, `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs`, and `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs`; targeted `rch` e2e tests for that slice passed earlier.
- Fresh canonical `release-perf` hot-profile replay for `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` / `frankensqlite` / `c8` / MVCC is currently blocked by `rch`'s 300s external timeout. I hit the same boundary twice on worker `vmi1167313`: the run gets through project-crate compilation but is killed before artifact sync, so there is still no new pack under `artifacts/perf/20260318_hot_profile_disjoint_c8_mvcc_telemetry`.
- While that evidence gate is blocked, I took one bounded code lever on the concurrent commit path itself: `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs` now carries `held_lock_pages` through prepare/finalize and swaps the begin-concurrent commit/abort/finalize cleanup path from scan-based `lock_table.release_all(txn_id)` to exact `lock_table.release_set(...)` whenever the handle/prepared state is available, with `release_all` retained only as the safety fallback when the handle cannot be recovered during prepare failure paths.
- Reason for the lever: FrostyBirch's code triage was correct that begin-concurrent still exercised the O(65536 + shard scan) `release_all` path even though `lifecycle.rs` already documents and uses exact lock-set release. Archived deep perf still does NOT sample `release_all` as a named top cycle sink (it shows up only at 0.00%), so this remains a fixed-cost hypothesis rather than a fully proven top hotspot; the patch is intentionally narrow and reversible.
- Added fresh unit coverage in `begin_concurrent.rs` so prepare/finalize preserve and release held-lock pages separately from staged write-set pages; targeted `rch` MVCC tests are running now.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:07:00Z"},{"id":2459,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18, pass 2):
- I re-audited the current MVCC exact-release patch in `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs` with fresh eyes. One concrete issue turned up in the newly added tests: the extra "held-lock-only" page had been modeled via `record_write_witness(WitnessKey::Page(...))`, which polluted the SSI write-witness plane instead of representing a pure extra held lock.
- Fixed: the tests now acquire the extra page in the lock table and mark only `ensure_page_state(page).held_lock = true`, so the scenario actually exercises `held_lock_pages` as a lock-only lane distinct from `write_set_pages`.
- The targeted `rch` MVCC tests still cannot reach this file because remote compilation is currently blocked earlier by unrelated `crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/uring.rs` failures (`linux-asupersync-uring` compile_error and missing methods). That means validation status for this bead remains code-reviewed + formatted, but not freshly green under cargo.
- Evidence status is unchanged on the perf side: canonical c8 `release-perf` hot-profile replay is still blocked by the `rch` 300s external timeout, so the best measured near-term E2.1 evidence remains the landed VDBE/e2e telemetry plus code-path inspection of begin-concurrent cleanup.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.2.2","title":"E2.2: fused prepared-DML transaction-entry path with one explicit fallback boundary","description":"## Goal
Turn the generic split-phase transaction idea into the concrete fused prepared-DML entry path that the latest measurements say FrankenSQLite is missing. The target is the ordinary prepared INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE common path, especially the weak `c1/c4` cells where transaction-boundary proof work currently dominates row execution.

## Why This Matters Now
Recent local `release-perf` artifacts show that the weak path is still dominated by boundary/control work rather than useful body execution. The same runs that improved pager begin behavior only modestly still show repeated prepared-schema and publication refresh activity, which means we are likely paying to establish overlapping truths multiple times per operation.

This bead therefore stops being a generic split-phase placeholder and becomes the home for one very specific mission: fuse schema freshness, publication binding, WAL/pager visibility binding, and autocommit begin into one transaction-entry proof object with one explicit fallback boundary.

## Scope
1. Measure exactly where the common prepared DML path duplicates refresh or binding work.
2. Define the fused transaction-entry ticket and invalidation rules.
3. Implement the fast path in `Connection::execute_prepared_with_params` and the downstream entry plumbing.
4. Verify that the fused path improves the weak cells without regressing correctness or concurrent-writer behavior.

## Non-Goals
- This is not a license to weaken schema invalidation, WAL generation safety, or visibility correctness.
- This is not a general-purpose HTM or lock-elision bead.
- This is not a parser or VDBE rewrite bead.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The common prepared DML path is measured and decomposed well enough to show exactly which begin/schema/publication checks are duplicated today.
2. A fused entry design exists with explicit ticket contents, validation points, and invalidation triggers.
3. The implementation removes redundant common-path proof work without weakening correctness, crash safety, or the default concurrent-writer model.
4. Canonical-matrix before/after artifacts exist for both single-writer and MVCC modes on the weak cells the change is meant to improve.
5. Fallback behavior remains explicit, logged, and testable rather than implicit in control-flow sprawl.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- If this bead lands correctly, it should make the engine feel more like a direct execution machine and less like a framework that re-proves the same invariants at every layer boundary.
- Keep causal attribution clean: one fast-path lever at a time, with before/after artifacts.

Operator sequencing:
- first actionable child: `bd-db300.5.2.2.1`
- then `bd-db300.5.2.2.2`, `bd-db300.5.2.2.3`, and `bd-db300.5.2.2.4` in order
- do not treat this container bead as the first implementation target; it closes when the child proof stack is green and the matrix evidence is archived","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:18:55.072106265Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:23:22.261143331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","concurrency","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-e","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.2.2.1","title":"E2.2.a: boundary-duplication census for prepared DML begin/schema/publication work","description":"## Goal
Build the exact duplication census for the common prepared DML path so the next optimization steps are grounded in evidence instead of hunches.

## Background
Current weak-cell artifacts imply that prepared DML still re-pays overlapping costs around schema freshness, publication binding, pager visibility refresh, and autocommit begin. Before changing control flow further, we need a mechanically trustworthy decomposition of how often each proof step happens and how much wall time, cycles, and instructions it consumes.

## Scope
Instrument and attribute the common prepared DML path for at least these operations:
- `refresh_prepared_schema_state`
- `try_refresh_prepared_metadata_if_stale`
- `bind_pager_publication`
- `refresh_memdb_if_stale_with_publication`
- `ensure_autocommit_txn_*` transaction-entry work
- pager begin-side committed-state refresh
- commit-side publication or fingerprint refresh bookkeeping

## Deliverables
1. Counters and phase timings in the hot-path profile snapshot or equivalent structured telemetry.
2. A per-operation trace contract that makes it obvious when the same transaction pays multiple overlapping refresh or binding costs.
3. Artifact packs for canonical weak and strong cells: `c1`, `c4`, and `c8` on the main workload families in both single-writer and MVCC modes.
4. A short conclusion naming which duplicate costs are real and therefore safe to collapse in follow-on beads.
5. A dedicated operator entrypoint such as `scripts/verify_e2_2_boundary_duplication.sh` that reruns the census on the chosen canonical cells and writes replayable artifact manifests.

## Required Verification and Logging
- Unit tests for counter accounting and phase attribution so instrumentation itself cannot silently drift.
- Integration tests proving the census survives prepared statement reuse, autocommit boundaries, explicit transactions, and schema invalidation events.
- Structured logs and profile fields including at minimum `trace_id`, `scenario_id`, `prepared_stmt_id`, `schema_refresh_count`, `publication_bind_count`, `begin_refresh_count`, `commit_refresh_count`, `entry_path`, and per-phase elapsed nanoseconds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The instrumentation is cheap enough to leave available for comparison runs.
- Results clearly identify duplicated begin/schema/publication work in the common path or prove that duplication is not the dominant issue.
- The output is specific enough that later beads can say exactly which counters should fall after their changes.
- This bead does not intentionally change runtime behavior beyond observability.
- The script and artifacts are sufficient for another agent to reproduce the census without re-reading this bead's rationale.

## Fallback / Decision Rule
If the census shows that duplication is not real or not material, explicitly record that and re-rank effort toward other matrix bottlenecks instead of cargo-culting the fused-path design.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Instrumentation covers the full prepared DML entry path and emits stable counter names across single-writer and MVCC modes.

3. `scripts/verify_e2_2_boundary_duplication.sh` or an equivalent named entrypoint exists and writes replayable artifact manifests for the canonical cells.
4. Structured logs expose `trace_id`, `scenario_id`, `prepared_stmt_id`, phase counts, phase timings, and `entry_path` so duplication can be diagnosed mechanically.

","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:56:25Z"},{"id":2526,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.2.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Closure-ready evidence (2026-03-23)

### Files changed
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`: Added 3 counters: `FSQLITE_BEGIN_REFRESH_COUNT`, `FSQLITE_COMMIT_REFRESH_COUNT`, `FSQLITE_MEMDB_REFRESH_COUNT` + wiring to `HotPathProfileSnapshot` + reset.
- `crates/fsqlite-core/tests/boundary_duplication_census.rs`: 5 tests (C1-C5).

### Run
```
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pane1-e221 cargo test -p fsqlite-core --test boundary_duplication_census -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture
```

### Result: 5 passed, 0 failed

### Census scorecard (100 file-backed prepared INSERTs, WAL autocommit)
| Counter | Total | Per-stmt | Duplicate? |
|---------|-------|----------|-----------|
| schema_refresh | 100 | 1.00 | YES — collapse with begin |
| publication_bind | 100 | 1.00 | YES — collapse with begin |
| begin_refresh | 100 | 1.00 | YES — collapse with schema |
| commit_refresh | 100 | 1.00 | No — needed per commit |
| memdb_refresh | 0 | 0.00 | No — correctly skipped |
| bg_status | 100 | 1.00 | YES — collapse with begin |

### Conclusion
Duplication IS real: schema_refresh + publication_bind + begin_refresh fire independently per autocommit statement. In explicit transactions, begin/commit refresh correctly drops to 0. The fused-entry path (bd-db300.5.2.2.2) should collapse schema_refresh + publication_bind + begin_refresh into one ticket when schema is unchanged.
